# WaterWise Enterprise

> Locally owned water catchment service company on the Big Island of Hawaii, based in Hawaiian Paradise Park (Puna). We install, clean, maintain, and repair rainwater catchment systems for off-grid homes across East Hawaii.

Contact: 808-765-7234 · waterwise.ent@gmail.com
Service area: Hawaiian Paradise Park, Pahoa, Keaau, Mountain View, Volcano, and Hilo, Hawaii.

## Services
- [Tank Installation](https://waterwiseent.com/services/tank-installation): Complete catchment system builds — tank, plumbing, gutters, and sediment/carbon/UV filtration designed for Hawaii's heavy rainfall. Typical cost $7,000–$20,000.
- [Tank Cleaning](https://waterwiseent.com/services/tank-cleaning): Deep cleaning — vacuum sediment, scrub walls, sanitize. Recommended every 2–3 years for most Puna homes.
- [Repairs & Emergency](https://waterwiseent.com/services/catchment-repairs): Leaks, pumps, pressure, failed filters, and same-day/next-day "no water" emergencies.
- [Roof Washing](https://waterwiseent.com/services/roof-washing): Catchment-safe removal of VOG, moss, and buildup — a cleaner roof means cleaner water.
- [Monthly Maintenance Plans](https://waterwiseent.com/services/maintenance-plans): Scheduled filter changes, water checks, and priority service.
- [Consultation & Upgrades](https://waterwiseent.com/services/catchment-consultation): Education-first walkthrough for new owners; first-flush diverters, UV, and filtration upgrades.

## Service areas
- [Hawaiian Paradise Park](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/hawaiian-paradise-park)
- [Pahoa](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/pahoa)
- [Keaau](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/keaau)
- [Mountain View](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/mountain-view)
- [Volcano](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/volcano)
- [Hilo](https://waterwiseent.com/service-area/hilo)

## Key facts
- Is catchment water safe to drink? Yes, with proper multi-stage filtration (sediment, carbon, UV).
- How often should a catchment tank be cleaned? A full deep clean every 2–3 years; filters every 3–6 months in Hawaii's climate.
- Licensed, insured, and locally owned in Hawaiian Paradise Park.
